title: Nursery Rhyme. DCXXXIII. Relics.
content:
[The following is quoted in the song of Mad Tom.]

        The man in the moon drinks claret,
            But he is a dull Jack-a-Dandy;
        Would he know a sheep's head from a carrot,
            He should learn to drink cider and brandy.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. DCXXXIII. Relics." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
